shows.title: Natural Remedies / Understanding Animal Behavior
shows.first_half: First Half: Dr. Drew Peterson will discuss the many ways we can use natural remedies to address ailments and health issues, in addition to modern medicine. Dr. Drew believes that supplementation and natural remedies are essential for everyday health.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Master dog trainer Jesse Sternberg will discuss practical approaches to understanding animal behavior and why he believes emotions play a key role across all species. He suggests that dogs often respond to our fear, anger, or confusion before they respond to commands, and that their behavior can shift when we steady our breathing and calm our minds.

shows.title: Timothy Leary & the Occult / Lake Superior Mysteries
shows.first_half: First Half: Author Joseph Flatley will share his research on psychologist and writer Timothy Leary, examining Leary’s lesser-known interest in Western esoteric traditions alongside his well-known advocacy for psychedelic drugs. Flatley will explore why some came to view Leary as a hero of the American consciousness movement.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Researcher of the strange, Chad Lewis, will explore the legends surrounding the rugged shores and vast waters of Lake Superior. He’ll recount stories of ghostly ships and mermaid lore, along with reports of UFOs, sea serpents, cursed locations, and wandering spirits.

shows.title: Cybercrime & Nancy Guthrie Case / Phoenix Lights
shows.guests: Eric O’Neill, Lynne D. Kitei  M.D.
shows.first_half: First Half: Former FBI counterterrorism operative Eric O’Neill will explore cybercrime and the mechanics of deception. He’ll also offer his perspective on the troubling details surrounding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, from the disabled doorbell camera, pacemaker data, to the Bitcoin ransom demands.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Witness and documentarian Dr. Lynne Kitei will discuss the mass UFO sighting known as the Phoenix Lights, reported over at least four states for over a dozen hours on March 13, 1997. She'll revisit the event and address what she says are persistent misunderstandings and misinformation surrounding the historic sighting, as well as riveting new updates.

shows.title: Friday the 13th Superstitions / Open Lines
shows.first_half: Joining guest host Connie Willis (info), Dr. Leo Ruickbie, author of seven books spanning subjects from Renaissance magic and First World War superstition to ghost hunting, witchcraft, and life after death, explains why people get hesitant and anxious about Friday the 13th. Followed by Open Lines in the latter half. Give Connie a call and discuss whatever is on your mind!
